citation.description: test_close_profile_accepts_a_planning_review asserts valid=True, verdict='certified', phase_status='compatible' with enforce_plan_material_pins=True and a planning-phase manifest at CLOSE profile. The attest.py compute_validity function (lines 527-548) applies review_phase_status uniformly for all profiles including CLOSE, with no CLOSE-specific override remaining.

criteria.criterion: Under `enforce_plan_material_pins=true`, `close_plan_review_gate_check` returns `ok=true, verdict="certified"` for an unchanged planning-phase attestation.

citation.description: Material-edit invalidation (lines 677-696) and pin enforcement (lines 700-706) apply regardless of profile (DEFAULT/CLOSE/DRIFT_REFRESH). The dogfood test test_in_progress_self_edit_requires_fresh_execution_review_before_close (lines 527-567) confirms material drift still blocks close.

criteria.criterion: A plan/pin change during execution still invalidates at close (material/pin drift), forcing a re-review — unchanged.

citation.description: review_phase_status returns 'incompatible' when current_phase=='planning' and signed_phase=='execution' (line 75). The parametrized test at test_plan_review_phase_validity.py line 44 encodes this row: ('planning', 'execution', 0.8, False, 'incompatible').

The doc explicitly states: 'The CLOSE profile uses this same table (it is not stricter on phase): a planning (or legacy-planning) attestation certifies close for an execution-phase ticket. The close gate's purpose is to catch a plan that changed during execution — enforced by own-material and pin drift, which still invalidate at close — not to compel a fresh execution-phase review when the plan is unchanged.'

criteria.criterion: `docs/plan-review-gate.md` states the CLOSE profile uses the same compatibility table.

citation.description: test_planning_phase_review_certifies_close_through_real_cli is a new end-to-end test asserting that an unchanged planning-phase attestation certifies close (check['ok'] is True, check['verdict']=='certified'). The unit test test_close_profile_accepts_a_planning_review (test_plan_review_phase_validity.py lines 67-88) also asserts the documented behavior. No test asserting the old incompatible-phase override for CLOSE remains.

criteria.criterion: Tests that encoded the undocumented override are inverted to the documented behavior.
